frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Sequential sampling drift-diffusion framework adapted to value-based choice

frameworks.name: value construction by deliberation
frameworks.one_line: Subjective values are built during sampling, not stably recalled

frameworks.name: stable-value with uncorrelated noise
frameworks.one_line: Standard BEA assumption that value noise is temporally independent

core_question: Are subjective values in value-based decisions stable with uncorrelated noise, or are they constructed and revalued across a session?
key_assumptions: Choices and response times reflect a sequential sampling process
Internal states of the decision maker can change across trials
Value-based decisions need not share perceptual decisions' i.i.d. noise assumption
manipulations: Compared explicitly stated values to Reval-derived dynamic values
Compared BEA models with independent vs. correlated successive evidence samples
key_findings: Subjective item values change significantly during a short experimental session
Reval values predict choice and response time better than stated values
Reval values better explain vmPFC BOLD activity
Revaluation also emerges in BEA models with non-independent evidence samples
interpretations: Revaluation is a byproduct of value construction during deliberation
Serial dependencies in value-based choice arise from temporally correlated sampling, not from corrupted stable values
